title: Fantasy Borough 2

jokes.text: What is this, eh... Fantasy Island? Well, actually, Latka, there really isn't a Fantasy Island.
jokes.setup: Latka innocently asks about Fantasy Island after seeing Herve Villechaize photos
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact correction that destroys the magic
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific moment - Latka's naivety meets deadpan reality check

jokes.text: You're never gonna believe who I had in my cab last night! Hey, that's Herve Villechaize.
jokes.setup: Driver builds excitement about an unbelievable celebrity encounter
jokes.punchline: It's Herve Villechaize, a very niche Fantasy Island celebrity
jokes.explanation: Simple setup with mild payoff - the celebrity is notable but not huge, creating modest comedic deflation

jokes.text: You seem to have a perspective on world events that somehow eludes the average man. You're no slouch yourself.
jokes.setup: Elaborate compliment about worldly perspective
jokes.punchline: Casual, understated return compliment
jokes.explanation: Mild comedic contrast between formal and casual speech patterns

jokes.text: Halt. I have other dreams, too.
jokes.setup: Dramatic 'Halt' suggests serious military/authority fantasy
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of having multiple dreams deflates the drama
jokes.explanation: Nice comedic puncturing of dramatic moment with mundane follow-up

jokes.text: Wow! You guys are funny-lookin'.
jokes.setup: Context suggests this is a fantasy sequence where someone important arrives
jokes.punchline: Blunt, unfiltered observation about appearance
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected honesty - breaks fantasy convention with reality

jokes.text: Here come da plane, boss. You like it? That's fine. That was you.
jokes.setup: Latka does impression for Herve Villechaize
jokes.punchline: Awkward approval and confirmation it was an impression
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Latka's earnest impression attempt with awkward interaction

jokes.text: Don't you know what Hollywood's like? Right now, that guy's out there surrounded by perverts, weirdos, and human garbage. Makes me glad I didn't go into show business.
jokes.setup: Describing Hollywood as filled with terrible people
jokes.punchline: Louie saying this makes him glad he didn't go into show business (when he's arguably worse)
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - complete lack of self-awareness while describing people exactly like himself

jokes.text: I've tried to fantasize. I've tried to think up some great situation, but somehow, something always goes wrong.
jokes.setup: Alex admits he can't fantasize properly
jokes.punchline: Even his fantasies go wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Alex's overthinking nature extends even to fantasies

jokes.text: Has anyone ever told you before that the back of your head is quite attractive. Thank you. The front's nice too. That's something to look forward to.
jokes.setup: Compliment about back of head, then front
jokes.punchline: Alex's literal interpretation as something to anticipate
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'look forward to' - shows Alex's wit even in fantasy

jokes.text: I've always heard how rude and abrupt cabbies were. You're not that way at all. Well, the evening is still young.
jokes.setup: Woman compliments Alex on not being rude like other cabbies
jokes.punchline: Alex's self-deprecating warning that he might still turn rude
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor - Alex acknowledging cabbie stereotypes while being charming

jokes.text: Feeling a little warm? Ever since I got into this cab. I can turn the heat down. I don't think you could.
jokes.setup: Discussion about temperature in cab
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about heat/attraction that Alex misses literally
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre setup, fairly predictable execution

jokes.text: So, tell me, uh, cookie... what brings you to New York? I'm here with my husband. Son of a... She's married?!
jokes.setup: Alex getting intimate with fantasy woman
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's married, Alex breaks character
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Alex's character flaw - even in fantasy, his conscience interferes

jokes.text: Oh, Alex, are you over there trying to fantasize? Ah, no, I told you I don't do that kind of stuff.
jokes.setup: Alex caught drifting off
jokes.punchline: Denial while obviously fantasizing
jokes.explanation: Standard caught-in-the-act denial, fits character but not exceptional

jokes.text: Hey, Alex, if you're fantasizing about a woman, make her single. Well, it's not that easy, you know. I mean, you just can't imagine her single, and she comes out single, can you?
jokes.setup: Advice to make fantasy woman single
jokes.punchline: Alex's overthinking the logistics of fantasy control
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific absurdity - Alex applies logic to fantasy, missing the point entirely

jokes.text: So you're married, huh? No, not really. I just said that. Why? I was afraid you'd come on to me. Well... why are you admitting that now? I decided I'd take my chances.
jokes.setup: Woman retracts marriage claim, explains she was being cautious
jokes.punchline: Now willing to risk Alex coming on to her
jokes.explanation: Decent reversal that moves fantasy forward, though fairly conventional

jokes.text: Would you like to stop someplace and have a cup of coffee? Oh, I'd love that.
jokes.setup: Romantic tension building
jokes.punchline: Alex's incredibly tame suggestion of coffee
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex character moment - even in fantasy, he's wonderfully modest and proper

jokes.text: Don't worry about it. I couldn't stop now if I had to. Stop. I can't. I can't. Stop!
jokes.setup: Fantasy moving toward intimacy, passenger saying not to worry
jokes.punchline: Alex literally can't stop his cab, crashes it in his own fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy concept - Alex's anxiety manifests as losing control of his own fantasy cab. Perfect character integration and escalation

jokes.text: I can't even be trusted behind the wheel of my own fantasy.
jokes.setup: Alex's fantasy goes wrong with accident
jokes.punchline: Self-aware observation about failing at his own fantasy
jokes.explanation: Excellent line - perfect metaphor for Alex's character, highly quotable, great economy

jokes.text: Now, now, just relax... and remember that it's your fantasy, and in her eyes, you can't do anything wrong. Yeah. You're right. I just hope she's still alive.
jokes.setup: Encouraging Alex that nothing can go wrong in fantasy
jokes.punchline: Alex's concern about woman surviving the accident
jokes.explanation: Good dark turn - Alex's practical concerns invade even fantasy romance

jokes.text: You're not going to get into any trouble, are you? No, I have a very understanding dispatcher.
jokes.setup: Woman worried about Alex getting in trouble
jokes.punchline: Alex calling Louie 'understanding'
jokes.explanation: Good irony - anyone who knows Louie would find this hilarious

jokes.text: What's the matter? I just realized something. I don't even know your name. Does it matter? How am I going to tell my diary about you?
jokes.setup: Romantic moment interrupted by practical concern
jokes.punchline: Alex's endearing worry about diary documentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex moment - sweet, neurotic concern about record-keeping during romance

jokes.text: Tawny. My favorite name. I'm Alex. My favorite name.
jokes.setup: Name exchange with romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: Exact parallel responses showing fantasy perfection
jokes.explanation: Simple parallel structure joke about fantasy wish fulfillment

jokes.text: A double-dip Rocky Road ice cream cone with a cherry on top. Right. How did...? Uncle Alex?!
jokes.setup: Romantic woman shares childhood memory about uncle
jokes.punchline: Alex realizes he's the uncle, fantasy becomes incestuous
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - perfect Alex catastrophe, expertly built up and devastatingly executed

jokes.text: How's Aunt Rose? Fine. Cousin Harry? Great. Brother Bill? Wonderful. Your daughter? Europe.
jokes.setup: Awkward family catch-up in destroyed fantasy
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact family updates after romantic disaster
jokes.explanation: Excellent follow-through - maintains absurd family reunion tone after fantasy destruction

jokes.text: Me? What would I need a fantasy for? I'm usually the object of other peoples' fantasies.
jokes.setup: Asked if he has fantasies
jokes.punchline: Claims to be the object of others' fantasies
jokes.explanation: Pure Louie delusion - perfectly in character, delivers on expected arrogance

jokes.text: He keeps that thermostat set right where it's supposed to be... 28 degrees.
jokes.setup: Praise for Louie's energy conservation
jokes.punchline: Impossible 28-degree setting
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail in Louie's power fantasy - extreme temperature as normal

jokes.text: Imagine how tough it would be being the only rich man left in the country.
jokes.setup: Discussing Depression hardships
jokes.punchline: Sympathy for the only rich person's burden
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation about fantasy power dynamic during Depression

jokes.text: That's a mighty nasty cough you got there, Rieger. Oh, it's nothing, sir. Just a touch of double pneumonia. It's going around.
jokes.setup: Concern about cough
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of 'double pneumonia' as minor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor - serious disease treated casually in fantasy power dynamic

jokes.text: That could be bronchitis on top of pneumonia. That's what Wheeler's got, and he's dying, aren't you, Wheeler? Yes, sir. That's the spirit.
jokes.setup: Concern about escalating illness
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation of dying with approval
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - cheerful acceptance of death in master-servant dynamic

jokes.text: Why are you dressed like that? Because it's so warm in here. You keep it at 85.
jokes.setup: Question about wife's revealing outfit
jokes.punchline: Contrast with garage being kept at 28 degrees - his home is 85
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the temperature control - Louie keeps workers freezing but his home tropical

jokes.text: Nardo, you're taking some of the surprise out of this.
jokes.setup: Elaine apparently revealing too much about what's coming
jokes.punchline: Louie breaking fourth wall about surprise
jokes.explanation: Mild meta moment - Louie controlling the narrative of his own fantasy

jokes.text: Benji... she's going through the roof. Snoopy... through the roof.
jokes.setup: Discussing other animal performers' success
jokes.punchline: Comparison to cartoon dog and movie dog earnings
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment - treating animal characters as business competition

jokes.text: Take a walk. All right, but just one.
jokes.setup: Firing Lassie (the dog)
jokes.punchline: Dog interprets 'take a walk' literally
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and wordplay - perfect dog behavior misunderstanding firing euphemism

jokes.text: Oh, well. A man can dream, can't he?
jokes.setup: End of Louie's elaborate fantasy sequence
jokes.punchline: Understated acknowledgment it was all fantasy
jokes.explanation: Nice punctuation to fantasy sequence - humble return to reality

jokes.text: But if I were to tell you my fantasy... it would knock you out.
jokes.setup: Elaine refuses to share fantasy, then teases about it
jokes.punchline: Claims it would be overwhelming
jokes.explanation: Standard tease setup - builds anticipation for what's coming

jokes.text: We always play cards. We play cards every day. That's all we do is play cards. Play cards.
jokes.setup: Complaint about repetitive card playing
jokes.punchline: Repetitive complaint mirrors the repetitive activity
jokes.explanation: Decent structural mirror - repetitive speech about repetitive activity

jokes.text: Elaine, we're trying to play cards here. Come on! Buzz off.
jokes.setup: Elaborate fantasy performance interrupted by mundane complaint
jokes.punchline: Elaine dismisses them to continue fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good reality-fantasy collision - mundane interruption of grand performance

jokes.text: Bounce! Kick!
jokes.setup: Continuing elaborate Broadway fantasy
jokes.punchline: Stage directions called out during performance
jokes.explanation: Basic visual comedy - calling out choreography during performance

jokes.text: Train! Pick me up! Turn me! Put me down! Kick!
jokes.setup: Fantasy performance continues with more elaborate staging
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and demanding choreography instructions
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the choreography commands - builds energy but not particularly clever

tmdb_overview: The cabbies continue with their fantasies--except Alex, who can't imagine a problem-free situation.
insight: Season 2 finale coasts on character comfort rather than sharp writing or memorable punchlines.
